volume.h1:
SOUTH INDIAN INSCRIPTIONS
Volume XXII
Part III

inscription.head:
No. 445.
(A.R. No. 445 of 1906)
COIMBATORE DISTRICT, GOBICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, DAṆĀYAKAṈKŌṬṬAI
Vīrabhadrasvāmi temple-on one of the pillars in the maṇḍapa
inscription.p:
This is in modern characters and is dated in Krōdhana, Tai. It
mentions some order (not clear) of the committee of the Vīrabhadrasvāmi
temple, during the regime (pārapatyam) of Nāgarasayyar, the agent of
Tāṇḍavamuttu-śeṭṭiyār.

inscription.head:
No. 446.
(A.R. No. 446 of 1906)
COIMBATORE DISTRICT, GOBICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, DAṆĀYAKAṈKŌṬṬAI,
On the dhvajastaṁbha of the Vīrabhadrasvāmi temple
Śaka 1669, Māgha śu 12
inscription.p:
Records the setting up of dīpada-vṛishabha-staṁbha in the temple
of Vīrabhadrasvāmi.

inscription.head:
No. 447.
(A.R. No. 447 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, KĪḺAḌI near ŚILAIMĀṈ
On the south wall of central shrine in the Arjunēśvara temple
Māṟavarman Tribhº Kulaśēkharadēva, Year 23
inscription.p:
Records that Kūpakarāya, the son of Maḻabarāya rebuilt the
temple and gave different names to the god and his consort in order
to secure merit of his parents. The temple is called Muchukunda-
tiruvirāmēśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Śrīkuntadēvich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam,
a brahmadēya to the east of the tank (kuḷakkīḻ) at Vēlūr in
Madurōdaya-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 448.
(A.R. No. 448 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, KĪḺAḌI near ŚILAIMĀṈ
On the north, west and south walls of the central shrine in the
Arjunēśvara temple
inscription.p: Mutilated. Seems to register a sale of land.

inscription.head:
No. 449.
(A.R. No. 449 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, KĪḺAḌI near ŚILAIMĀṈ
On the north, west and south walls of the central shrine in the
Arjunēśvara temple
Māṟavarmaṉ alias Tribhuvanachakravartin Kulaśēkharadēva, ‘who
was pleased to take every country, year-30+1
inscription.p:
Sale of land to Kūttaṉ Teṉṉāḍaṉ alias Tribhuvanaśiṅgadēva of
Kaṇṇaṉūr in Malai-maṇḍalam by the sabhā of Śrīkuntidēvi-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam, a brahmadēya to the east of the tank at Vēlūr in
Madurōdaya-vaḷanāḍu which was a dēvadāna of the temple of
Muḍivaḻaṅgu-Pāṇḍya-Īśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, refers to the executive
committee nirvvāhaka-sabhai of the village and to the 24th year (of
the king's reign).

inscription.head:
No. 450.
(A.R. No. 450 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, KĪḺAḌI near ŚILAIMĀṈ
On a stone set up near a channel.
Kulaśēkhara, Year 22
inscription.p: Records the construction of a channel.

inscription.head:
No. 451.
(A.R. No. 451 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, KĪḺAḌI near ŚILAIMĀṈ
Another stone set up near a channel
inscription.p:
Fragmentary and much damaged. Mentions a chaturvēdimaṅgalam
(name not clear) and one Gurukularāyaṉ. Seems to refer to the gift of
paddy to a deity (name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 452.
(A.R. No. 452 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, KĪḺAḌI NEAR ŚILAIMĀṈ
Stone set up in a field near the village
In late characters
Refers to the gift of Veṭṭumāvili.

No. 453.
(A.R. No. 453 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, ĀṈAIMALAI
At the entrance into the central shrine of the temple, right side.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 1.

inscription.head:
No. 454.
(A.R. No. 454 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, ĀṈAIMALAI
At the entrance into the central shrine of the temple, left side.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 2.

inscription.head:
No. 455.
(A.R. No. 455 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, ĀṈAIMALAI
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine of
the temple.
Kṛishṇarāya Śaka 1441, Pramādi
inscription.p:
Mentions Timma-bhūpa as the king's door-keeper and a certain
Madhurā-nātha as his agent.

inscription.head:
No. 456.
(A.R. No. 456 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, ĀṈAIMALAI
On a stone built into the floor of the platform in front of the shrine
of the goddess in the temple.
Śrīvallabha, Year 6
inscription.p:
Fragment. Mentions Kandāḍai Kāḷamēgha-bhaṭṭaṉ of the Āpastaṁbha-
sūtra.

inscription.head:
No. 457.
(A.R. No. 457 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, ĀṈAIMALAI (Ottakkaḍai)
Entrance into the natural cave known as Pañchapāṇḍava-paḍukkai.
inscription.p:
These (beds) (are the) gift of Nātaṉ (living in the) dormitory of
Kuṉṟattūr. (These were made by) Eri Aritaṉ Attuvāyi (and) Araṭṭa
Kāyipaṉ. Palaeographically the inscription can be assigned to c. 1st
century A.D.

l.ta: iva kuṉṟattūruṟaiyuḷ nātaṉ tāṉa tāṉa eri aritaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 458.
(A.R. No. 458 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, TIRUMANGALAM TALUK, KŌḌIKUḶAM
On the Āṉaimalai hill close to the Vēdanārāyaṇaperumāḷ temple.
Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśēkharadēva, Year 35
inscription.p:
Registers the sale of land to the god Sēnāpati-āḻvār in the
Kāḷamēgham (temple).

l.ta:
tevaṟku yāṇṭu muppattaiñcāvatu āṉi mātam nam

inscription.head:
No. 459.
(A.R. No. 459 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, KŌḌIKUḶAM
On a stone set up in a field near the Vēdanārāyāṇa-perumāḷ temple
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the temple of Añjāda-Perumāḷ alias
Vēdanāthar situated on the banks of the Brahmatīrtha in the
Tuṅgavaṉam (forest) in Vēlaṅgāl-paṟṟu on the east side of Āṉaimalai,
together with its tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam, its flower gardens and its agarams
(brahmin villages) would be the place of asylum to the nāṭṭār of
Kalavaḻi and the Aiñjunilai-paṟṟu. Aṉaimalai is said to be situated to
the north of Tirumōgūr in Teṉpaṟappu-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 460.
(A.R. No. 460 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, MĀṄGUḶAM, (Ariṭṭāppaṭṭi)
On four boulders on the Kaḻugumalai hill
inscription.p:
This was dedicated to Kaṇiynanta, the monk (living) yonder and
this monastary was made by Chaṭikaṉ, the father of Iḷañchatikaṉ and
brother-in-law (sālakaṉ) of Neṭiñchaḻiyaṉ. Palaeographically this record
may be assigned to c. 4th century B.C.

inscription.head:
No. 461.
(A.R. No. 461 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, MĀṄGUḶAM, (Ariṭṭappāṭṭi)
On four boulders on the Kaḻugumalai hill
inscription.p:
It states that this (the bed) was caused to be given by
Chantaritaṉ. The record may be assigned to c. 4th century B.C.

inscription.head:
No. 462.
(A.R. No. 462 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, MĀṄGUḶAM, (Ariṭṭappāṭṭi)
On four boulders on the Kaḻugumalai hill
inscription.p:
It states that the members of the mercantile guild of Veḷaṟai
gave this. The record may be assigned to c. 4th century B.C.

inscription.head:
No. 463-464.
(A.R. No. 463 & 464 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, MĀṄGUḶAM, (Ariṭṭappāṭṭi)
On four boulders on the Kaḻugumalai hill
inscription.p:
It states that the son of Kāḻitika Antai, a merchant prince
(Kāviti) of the merchant guild of Veḷaṟai caused this lattice (piṇa) to
be given to Kaṇi-i-nata, the monk. The record may be assigned to c.
4th century B.C.

inscription.head:
No. 465.
(A.R. No. 465 of 1906)
MADURAI DISTRICT, MADURAI TALUK, MĀṄGUḶAM, (Ariṭṭappaṭṭi)
On four boulders on the Kaḻugumalai hill
inscription.p:
Refers to the gift to Kaṇiynanta (the monk) (āsiriyika) (living)
yonder (uvaṉ). This monastery was caused to be given by Kaṭalaṉ
Vaḻuttiy, an officer (paṇa-aṉ) under Neḍuñchaḻiyaṉ. In characters of
c. 4th century B.C.

inscription.head:
No. 466.
(A.R. No. 466 of 1906)
Cuddapah District, Proddutur Taluk, Prodduṭūru
On four faces of a pillar set up near Mallāreḍḍi's house
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. X, No. 595.

inscription.head:
No. 467.
(A.R. No. 467 of 1906)
Cuddapah District, Proddutur Taluk, Prodduṭūru
On a slab built into the wall of another house.
(Fragmentary)
Śaka 1331, Virōdhi.
inscription.p: This record refers to the titles of a Vijayanagara king.

Worn out. Seems to record that the image of Hanumant was set
up by Śimaya-reḍḍi, son of Āmali-reḍḍi and registers the grant of wet
lands.

l.te: nēṭi sarvajitu saṁvatsara śrāvaṇa bahuḷa 5 ·ā caṁ
gānu celliṁcēdi-maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 469.
(A.R. No. 469 of 1906)
Cuddapah District, Proddutur Taluk, Kāmanūru
On a stone unearthed in the bed of the stream
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 610.

Records contributions made by merchants at fixed rates on
articles of merchandise for the benefit of the temple of
Kōdaṇḍaraghunāthasvāmi at Duvvūru.

inscription.head:
No. 471.
(A.R. No. 471 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PRODDUTUR TALUK, JILLĒḶḶA
On a slab set up near a water course by the side of the road.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 173.

inscription.head:
No. 472.
(A.R. No. 472 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, RAJAMPET TALUK, ṬAṄGUṬŪRU,
On a slab set up in front of Madanagōpāla temple
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 162.

Registers the gift of garuḍa-staṁbha pillar to the temple of Śrī
Madanagōpālasvāmi of Taṅgaṭūru by Veṅkaṭa Chinapāpi-reḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 474.
(A.R. No. 474 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PRODDUTUR TALUK, ARAKAṬAVĒMULA
On two sides of a pillar set up in the court yard of the
Vishṇu temple
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 604.

Refers to Kāmarusugāri-Timmayya and Brāhmala Muḍiyam, the
ancient name of Peddamuḍiyam in Jammalamadugu, also Nandyāla in
Rēnāḍu.

inscription.p:
Registers the gift of land by the mahājanas of Chavuḍūru to
Peda Timmarasayya Kāmarasu, perhaps identical with the person
mentioned in inscription No. 475.

(A.R. No. 477 of 1906)

On the pillar set up in the courtyard of the Chennakēśava temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 81.

inscription.head:
No. 478.
(A.R. No. 478 of 1906)

On two faces of a slab lying in the court yard of the
Chennakēśavasvāmin temple
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 62.

inscription.head:
No. 479.
(A.R. No. 479 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PRODDUTUR TALUK, GŌPAVARAM
On two faces of a slab set up in the middle of the village
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 105.

Fragment. Mentions Kandhāḷa Dēvarājāchārya of the Vādhūla-
gōtra and his son. The former is credited with the title
Vēdamārgapratishṭāchārya. Also mentions the alternative name for
Gōpavaram. In characters of the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 481.
(A.R. No. 481 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PRODDUTUR TALUK, GŌPAVARAM
On a mutilated slab set up in the middle of the village.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XVI, No. 127

Registers the gift of land to the temple of Chennakēśava-perumāḷ
at Śeṭipale by the mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāju Rāyanarāju Koṇḍayyadēva-
mahārāju. The gift was obtained from Aḷiyarāmarāju as uṁbaḷi.

No. 483.
(A.R. No. 483 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PRODDUTUR TALUK, CHAUḌŪRU
On a slab lying in the Bhīmēśvara temple
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, Part I, No. 402

inscription.head:
No. 484.
(A.R. No. 484 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PRODDUTUR TALUK, CHAUḌŪRU
On a pillar lying near the Māriyamma temple
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 609

inscription.head:
No. 485.
(A.R. No. 485 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, GAṆḌIKŌṬA
On the south wall of the prākāra of the ruined temple of
Raṅganāyaka
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 224

inscription.head:
No. 486.
(A.R. No. 486 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, GAṆḌIKŌṬA
On the south wall of the prākāra of the ruined temple of
Raṅganāyaka
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 144

Registers the gift to Reghunāyaka temple at Ghaṇḍikōṭa by a
merchant named Chamaṭaya, the son of Bula Timmayya.

Records the remission of taxes in favour of the fishermen living
in the new street at Kammadurgamu by the mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Nārasiṁha-rājayyadēva-mahārāju of Nandyāla. In characters of the 16th
century A.D.

l.note: The four letters·āṣāḍa ba 13lū are written above the line.

inscription.p:
Records the gift of two gardens to the temple of Chaṁnarāya at
Nāgadhari by Kṛishṇappa Timmarāju Nāgarāju Veṅkaṭarāju Koṇḍrāju
of Āravīṭi for the merit of Raṅgapatirāju.

(A.R. No. 490 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, GAṆḌIKŌṬA
On a boulder near the water fall in the garden called Pārebhāg on
the bank of the Peṇṇār river at the foot of the hill.
(In Persian characters)

No. 491.
(A.R. No. 491 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, PULIVEṆḌLA
On a slab set up at the entrance of the Raṅganāthasvāmin temple
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 45

inscription.head:
No. 492.
(A.R. No. 492 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, PULIVEṆḌLA
On Two faces of a slab lying at the entrance of the Āñjanēyasvāmin
temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 110

Registers the gift of land probably situated in Puli-viṁdala by
the mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Jagatāpi Kaṁchiyyadēva-mahārāju to the goddess
Pulikumāramma.

No. 494.
(A.R. No. 494 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, PULIVEṆḌLA
On a slab near Govinda Rao's house
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 770

No. 495.
(A.R. No. 495 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, PULIVEṆḌLA
On a pillar lying near a well called the Kaṭṭabhāvi on the way to the
Mōpūru temple.

On a slab lying behind the eastern gōpura of the Bhairavēśvara
temple
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 28

No. 497.

On a stone lying behind the western gōpura of the temple of

inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 615

inscription.head:
No. 498.
(A.R. No. 498 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, MŌPŪR
On a slab set up in front of the central shrine of the Bhairavēśvara
temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 139

Registers the assignment of land by mahānāyaṅkāchārya Raṅgapatirāju
to the agriculturists (kāṁpulu) of Durgabīḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 499.
(A.R. No. 499 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, MŌPŪR
On a slab set up in front of the central shrine of the Bhairavēśvara
temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 91.

inscription.head:
No. 500.
(A.R. No. 500 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, MŌPŪR
On a slab lying near the Vibhūti-maṇḍapa of the Bhairavēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 2.

Damaged. Records the remission of taxes for the merit of Vīra
Narasiṁharāya and Sāḷuva Timmayya on the occasion of Vutthāna-
dvādaśi.

No. 502.
(A.R. No. 502 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, MŌPŪR
On a slab built into the floor at the entrance into the central shrine
of Bhairavēśvara temple.
Dundubhi, Vaiśākha śu. 15.
inscription.p:
Seems to register the gift of villages to the god Bhairavēśvara at
Mōpūru by Tamaṭam Veṅkaṭayya an officier of the mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Naṁdēla Timmarājayyadēva-mahārāju.

On the left side of the wall at the entrance into the maṇḍapa of the
shrine. Bhairavēśvara temple.

No. 505.
(A.R. No. 505 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, MŌPŪR
On the wall at the entrance into the maṇḍapa in front of the central
shrine of Bhairavēśvara temple.
Śaka 1295, Pramādi, Chaitra śu 5 = 1373 A.D., March 29
inscription.p:
Registers the transaction involving the sale of their shares among
the temple priests.

inscription.head:
No. 506.
(A.R. No. 506 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, MŌPŪR
On the right and left door posts of the entrance in to the maṇḍapa
in the temple of Bhairava.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 498.

Records the death of Avubalanatha, son of the mahāmaṇḍalēśvara,
Chāḷukyanārāyaṇa, Vēṅgī-Tribhuvanamalla, lord of Ayōdhya and worshipper
of the feet of Alāḷanātha, Pākarāju Pemmayadēva-mahārāju.

The hero had gone on an expedition to the Mārjavāḍi country
and died at Dhārātīrtha.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Records the sale of certain shares held by the priests
of the Bhairavēśvara temple at Mōpūru.

of their rights among the temple priests.

inscription.p:
It refers to some priests and to some transactions among their
shares.

(A.R. No. 514 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, MUTTUKŪRU
On a slab built into the platform in front of the
Chennakēśavasvāmi temple.

On the door-post of the entrance into the central shrine of the

No. 518.
(A.R. No. 518 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, MUTTUKŪRU
On a pillar lying of the Śiva temple
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 613.

inscription.head:
No. 519.
(A.R. No. 519 of 1906)
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT, PULIVENDLA TALUK, ULIMEḶḶA
On a slab set up in front of the Chennakēśavasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 37.

Records the reconstruction of a street and house to the west of
Narasiṁha temple at Kadiri in the name of Saṁggayadēva-mahārāju,
son of Chōḍarāyagurudēva-mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 521.
(A.R. No. 521 of 1906)
ANANTAPUR, DISTRICT, KADIRI TALUK, KADIRI
On four faces of a pillar lying at the western entrance into the
Lakshmīnarasiṁhasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, part II, No. 438

inscription.head:
No. 522.
(A.R. No. 522 of 1906)
ANANTAPUR, DISTRICT, KADIRI TALUK, KADIRI
On a slab set up near the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the
Lakshmīnarasiṁhasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 4.

Gives a Sanskrit verse in ślēsha composed by the Pradhāni
Sōvappagaḷu, on the occasion of the gift of an ornament to the temple
at Kadiri by Gōpaṇṇa, an official of Kumāra Kaṁpaṇṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 524.
(A.R. No. 524 of 1906)
ANANTAPUR DISTRICT, KADIRI TALUK, KADIRI
On a slab built into the floor of the court-yard of Lakshmīnarasiṁha
temple.
Khara, Mārgaśira, ba 10
inscription.p:
Records the grant of a charter (naṁmike śāsanamu) pertaining to
the remission of taxes on mānya lands held by the potters. (Kammaris)
by Dāḍināyani Gōppa-nāyaka, the kāryakarta of Jilleḷa.

l.te: śubhamastu | kharasaṁvatsara mā¨rgaśira ba 10 [śu ?] [||*] jilleḷa nārasi-
ri [vāṁ]ḍlaku naṁmikesi(śi)lāśāsanaṁ [|*] kadiri grāmāna mīku saḍicē

inscription.head:
No. 525-526.
(A.R. No. 525 and 526 of 1906)
ANANTAPUR DISTRICT, KADIRI TALUK, KADIRI
On a slab built into the floor of the kitchen in the
Lakshmīnarasiṁhasvāmi temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 83. and 24 respectively

Registers the grant of land to Appalāchārya in the Rāmānuja-
kūṭa of Kadiri-sthaḷa by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Sundara Abiḷarājaya. Also
provision was made for providing food-offerings to the god Nārasiṁha
and the Āḷvārs.

inscription.head:
No. 528.
(A.R. No. 528 of 1906)
ANANATAPUR DISTRICT, KADIRI TALUK, KADIRI
On the base of a big gōpura, right and left sides of
Lakshmīnarasiṁhasvāmi temple.
Date lost
inscription.p:
Mutilated. Gives the genealogy of the first Vijayanagara dynasty.
It mentions king Bukka and his minister Viṭṭhalāmātya, the son of
Dēvapāmātya of Bhārgavamuni-gōtra and seems to refer to the
construction of the gōpura probably by the latter (Viṭṭhala).

inscription.p: Incomplete. Records the gift by a merchant from Tāḍiparati.

l.te:
śubhamastu svastiśrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavaruṣaṁbulu 1451
·agunēṭi virōdhi saṁvatsaraṁ cayitra ba 5 sō śrīmatu catu¨rdaśa bhuvanēśvara
śṛṁgāra vārānni[dhi] ·akhilāṁḍa-

kōṭi brahmāṁḍanāyaka dēvadēvōttamula·ina kadiri śrīnārasiṁhya dēvuniki tāḍi
parati ·avubhaḷaseṭṭi koḍuku padmapālakṛṣṇamaseṭṭigāru kōṭi tirunāḷa
maṁḍapāna sānu • •

inscription.p:
Registers the gift of a dīpastaṁbha to the deity Narasiṁhadēva
of Kadiri by Nāḍōba, son of vāśakāra Lakshmaṇa Dāsari.
Damaged. Mentions the names of several seṭṭis.

Damaged. Seems to register the gift of a garden for the merit of
Avubaḷammagāru, mother of Kōnamarāju of Tippapalli.
In characters of 15th-16th centuries A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 533.
(A.R. No. 533 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, MADANAPALLE TALUK, PEDDATIPPASAMUDRAM
On a slab set up near the ruined Śiva temple in the Yēnugarāti-maḍi
field at the above place.
inscription.p: Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XIV, p. 192

inscription.head:
No. 534.
(A.R. No. 534 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, MADANAPALLE TALUK, PEDDATIPPASAMUDRAM
On a slab near the ruine Śiva temple in the Yēnugarātimaḍi field.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. IX, part I No. 302.

inscription.head:
No. 535.
(A.R. No. 535 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, MADANAPALLE TALUK, PEDDATIPPASAMUDRAM
On a slab in the field called Yēnugarātimaḍi.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. X No. 641.

inscription.head:
No. 536.
(A.R. No. 536 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, MADANAPALLE TALUK, PEDDATIPPASAMUDRAM
On a slab lying in a field in Pullagaṇṭivāripalle, a hamlet of the
village.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. X No. 637.

inscription.head:
No. 537.
(A.R. No. 537 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, MADANAPALLE TALUK, PEDDATIPPASAMUDRAM
In a maṇḍapa situated near a field at Pullagaṇṭivāripalle
Adhirājēndradēva-3rd Year (A.D. 1070)
inscription.p:
This is slightly damaged. It seems to record a grant of land to
the survivor of certain Rājēndraśōḻachcheṭṭi of Mēlai Mārā[ya*]pāḍi in
Koyyakurai-nāḍu, a division of Iraṭṭapāḍikoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, who probably
lost his life in the cause of the village.

edition.p: At the top of the inscription are seen the engravings.

inscription.p: Damaged. Mentions Nāgapa-voḍaya.

inscription.head:
No. 539.
(A.R. No. 539 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, MADANAPALLE TALUK, MORUSUPALLE
On a slab set up in a field near the Śiva temple.
inscription.p:
Fragment and mutilated. In early characters Except the imprecatory
portion other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 540.
(A.R. No. 540 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, PUṄGANŪR
On a broken slab lying on the tankbund to the west of the fort
Rājakēsarivarman Rājēndra-Chōḷadēva, year 3 (1072 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This is the same as Kulōttuṅga I. The inscription begins with a
short introduction Bhūmiyum-tiruvum etc. It registers an assignment of
land (under the orders of the king) by sēnāpati Rājēndraśōḻa-
Brahmādhirāja and the sabhā of Kuṅgaṉūr alias Vaḷavanārāyaṇa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puli-nāḍu, a division of Raṭṭapāḍikoṇḍaśōḻa-
maṇḍalam for the construction of a tank called the Rājēndraśōḻapperiyēri
in the village.

inscription.head:
No. 541.
(A.R. No. 541 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, PUṄGANŪR
Sōmēśvarasvāmi temple-On a slab set up in the courtyard
Rājakēsari Vīrarājēndradēva, 4th year (A.D. 1060)
inscription.p:
The inscription opens with a few birudas of the king in Sanskrit
after which it gives the introduction Vīramētuṇaiyāgavum, etc. It
registers a grant of 2800 kuḻi of wet land and 1400 kuḻi of dry land
under the name Parahita-viḷāgam for the various requirements of
worship and offerings to the images of Sōmāskanda called Vijayarāja-
viṭaṅka and of Naṭarāja called Śrīkaraṇa-viṭaṅka which had been
consecrated in the temple of Tirukkoḷḷīśvaram-uḍaiya-mahādēva at
Kuṅganūr alias Vaḷavanārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puli-nāḍu a
division of Perumbāṇappāḍi in Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-
maṇḍalam by Śrīkaṇṭaṉ Rudramūrtti alias Bhūpālaśikhāmaṇi-Viḻupparaiyaṉ,
a native of Śiṟuvēḷūr in Panaiyūr-nāḍu a division of Kshatriyaśikhāmaṇi-
vaḷanāḍu in Śōḻa-maṇḍalam. The sabhā of the place made a detailed
apportionment of paddy for these several items of expenditure.

inscription.head:
No. 542.
(A.R. No. 542 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, PUṄGANŪR
On a slab set up outside the Sōmēśvarasvāmin temple
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. IX, part I, No. 5.

inscription.head:
No. 543.
(A.R. No. 543 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHAḌAḶḶU
On a slab set up in a field infront of the village.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. IX, part I, No. 11.

inscription.head:
No. 544.
(A.R. No. 544 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, LAḌḌIGAM
On the gōpura in front, right of entrance of the Nīlakaṇṭēśvara temple
inscription.p:
This is a verse in praise of the sword of Adigaṉ called also
Adiyamāṉ Viḍugādaḻagiya Perumāḷ (the Chēra chief of Tagaḍūr) who
won a victory in battle against the Koṅgu army. His ensign of power
viz., a parasol (?) and a chāmara (flywhisk) are engraved below the
inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 545.
(A.R. No. 545 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, LAḌḌIGAM
On the same gōpura, left of entrance of the Nīlakaṇṭēśvara temple
inscription.p:
This is another verse in praise of Adigēśan, Adiyamāṉ Viḍu-
Kādaḻagiya Perumāḷ (of Tagaḍūr) who having swept over the country
of Teṉ-Koṅgu, made himself master of the Gaṅga country. Below the
inscription is engraved the figure of a parasol flanked on either side
with a chāmara (flywhisk).

inscription.head:
No. 546.
(A.R. No. 546 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, LAḌḌIGAM
On the south wall of the prākāra of the Nīlakaṇṭēśvara temple
Vikrama-Chōḷadēva-9th year (A.D. 1126)
inscription.p:
This is an unfinished inscription stopping with the mention of a
certain Rājamāṇikka-Viḻupparaiyar as a donor to the temple of
Iruṅgōḷīśvaram-uḍaiya-Mahādēvar at Kōyāṟṟūr alias Uttamaśōḻapuram.

inscription.head:
No. 547.
(A.R. No. 547 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, LAḌḌIGAM
On the north wall of the prākāra of the Nīlakaṇṭēśvara temple
inscription.p:
This consists of three verses in Sanskrit and two in Tamil
describing the fame and prowess of the Kēraḷa chief (or King)
Adhikēndra Vyāmukhaśravaṇōjvala. His regal emblems of a parasol and
a bow below it with what looking like chāmara (flywisk) flanking
either side of it are engraved below the inscription.

l.gr:
svastiśrī āyuṣmannadhikēndramāna nilaya tvatkirttimugdhāmbudhau
ttrailōkyam kalavōtapātramakhilammērurggu

ṭēṣu kaṭāyatē pariluḍanmēghāvalī pallavastatratya-
stanutē nirantaramiḷanmiyyāmatāmaryyamāi viraśrī laḷinatō-
yānamahita kṣmāpālacū-

ḍāmaṇiśrēṇivāraṇa vāraṇa kramavidhā vāḷānasvāla
drumaḥ śaśvattēdhikamāna śatru dhavaḷacphatrēndu bimbagraha
rāhurbāhu rasamhya sāhasa nidhirmdhattā-

ndharāmaṇḍalam . śrīmat kēraḷa vamśabhūṣaṇa
mitikhyātasya pṛdhvipatērvyāmuttaśravaṇōjvalasya
mahatō jaitrāsu dhātrāsuca nissāṇōndhata
bhīmanādamaniśanimma-

tvā mahābhōgina śśrimattōsya vipakṣarāja bhujaṅgā
nityā vināśaṅgatāḥ .

ceraṉatikaṉ tiruneṭumāl

kal . metiṉi nirveṇṭil viṭukātaḻakiyāṉ kotil
pukaḻatikaṉ koyāṟṟūrāti vaṭaviru-

ṅ koḷiccarattil vaitta cilai mārpiliṭa viruṅkoḷa

inscription.head:
No. 548.
(A.R. No. 548 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, LAḌḌIGAM
On the south wall of the shrine, right of entrance of the
Nīlakaṇṭēśvara temple
inscription.p:
This records a gift of three kāśu deposited with the priests by an
anonymous donor for burning three lamps during the services in the
temple of Iruṅgōḷīśvaram-uḍaiya-mahādēva at Kōyāṟṟūr alias
Uttamaśōḻapuram, with the ghee derived as monthly interest on the
amounts.

l.ta: iṭṭa aṉṟāṭu naṟkācu muṉṟum kācuvāyt tiṅ-
m polinta policaiyāl tiṅkaḷ mun nā-
ḻi neyyāl tiruviḷakku erikkak kaṭavār ik

inscription.head:
No. 549.
(A.R. No. 549 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, LAḌḌIGAM
Nīlakaṇṭēśvara temple-In the same place
Mahāmaṇḍlēśvara Uttama-Chōḷa-Gaṅga alias Sembagachchīpati Nāyaṉār
inscription.p:
This records the gift of a throne (dēvāsana) for seating the deity
in the temple of Iruṅgōḷīśvaram-uḍaiyār by a certain Kali Vayirāṇḍai
of Kōyāṟṟūr.

inscription.head:
No. 550.
(A.R. No. 550 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, LAḌḌIGAM
Nīlakaṇṭēśvara temple-In the same place
Mahāmaṇḍlēśvara Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa III-20th year (A.D. 1197)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 96 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Iruṅgōḷīśvaram-uḍaiya-mahādēva by a certain Pallavayaṉ
Pambaṉ alias Vīrarājēndra-Kavaraiśeṭṭi, a farmer of Kōyāṟṟūr alias
Uttamaśōḻapuram.

l.ta:
p perāṭāka celuttakkaṭavār maṉṟāṭi ceṉaiyum
comanum [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 551.
(A.R. No. 551 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, LAḌḌIGAM
On the south wall of the shrine, left of entrance of the
Nīlakaṇṭēśvara temple
Rājarājadēva (III)-9th year (A.D. 1224)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of money to the temple of Iruṅgōḷīśvaram-
uḍaiya-nāyanār at Kōyāṟṟūr alias Uttamaśōḻapuram in Vaḍapuli-nāḍu a
division of Perumbāṇappāḍi in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam by Akaḷaṅka-
Sīyagaṅgaṉ alias Śittavaḍadēvaṉ for the merit of the chief Uttamaśōḻagaṅgar
alias Veṭṭum Amarābaraṇar.

l.ta:
peru[m*]pāṇappāṭi vaṭapuli nāṭṭuk koyāṟṟūr āṉa
uttamacoḻapurattu iruṅko-

inscription.head:
No. 552.
(A.R. No. 552 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, LAḌḌIGAM
On the east wall of the shrine of the Nīlakaṇṭēśvara temple
inscription.p:
This is a Tamil verse in praise of Viḍugādaḻagiya-śīyaṉ of Teṉ-
Tagaḍai, whose enemies were the Kāḍava, the Magada and the Gaṅga.

l.ta:
veṟṟiyatikā viṭukātaḻakiyāy koṟṟavaṉe
teṉtakaṭaik kove nī ceṟṟanāṭ kāṭavaṉo mākataṉo
kaṅkaṉo veṅkāṉi loṭaval[l*]ā(rā)ro vurai .

inscription.head:
No. 553.
(A.R. No. 553 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, LAḌḌIGAM
On the south east, north and west walls of the shrine of the
Nīlakaṇṭēśvara temple
Rājakēsarivarman Chakravartin Kulōttuṅga Chōḷadēva I-
16th year (A.D. 1085)
inscription.p:
This begins with the introduction Pugaḻśūḻnda-puṇari, etc. It
states that while the king was seated in the Chitra-maṇḍapa (art
gallery ?) of his palace at Ayirattaḷi alias Āhavamallakulakālapuram he
issued orders granting as tax-free dēvadāṉa, the village of Mādamaṅgalam
in Puli-nāḍu, a division of Raṭṭapāḍikoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, for the
requirements of worship to the deity in the temple of Iruṅgōḷīśvaram-
uḍaiya-mahādēva at Kōyāṟṟūr alias Uṭṭamaśōḻapuram. which is stated to
have been built by Aḍavalāṉ Gaṅgaikoṇḍaśōḻan alias Iruṅgōḷaṉ. The
village granted was renamed Gaṅgaikoṇḍaśōḻanallūr with its former
tenants replaced by others. The deed is signed by a large number of
the king's officers headed by the Tirumandiravōlai Rāmaṉ Lakkuvaṇaṉ
alias Rājarāja-Pallavaraiyaṉ of Kappalūr and the Tirumandiravōlai-
nāyakam Toṇḍaimāṉ.

l.ta:
pukaḻcūḻnta puṇari akaḻ cūḻnta puviyiṟ
poṉnemiyaḷavun taṉnemi naṭappa viḷaṅku jayamakaḷai
yiḷaṅkopparuvattu cakkarakoṭṭattu vikkiramat

toṉnilaventar

inscription.p:
Damaged. In praise of a Bāṇa king. Other details are lost. In
characters of c. 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 555.
(A.R. No. 555 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, CHITTOOR TALUK, PUDUPAṬLA
On a pillar set up in front of Chauḍēśvarī temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, part I, No. 9.

inscription.head:
No. 556.
(A.R. No. 556 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, AGASTYAKOṆḌA
Agastyēśvara temple-On four tiers on the south, east and north walls
Rājakēsari Chakravartin Kulōttuṅga Chōḷadēva
29th year (1098 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Like No. 553 above this also begins with the introduction Pugaḻ-
sūḻnda-puṇari, etc. It registers an undertaking given by the residents of
Puli-nāḍu in Perumbāṇappāḍi a division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam to
pay all the rents and taxes due on the dēvadāna village Kāvaṉkuṭṭai
to the temple of Tiruvagattīśvaramuḍaiya Mahādēva at Kōyāṟṟūr alias
Uttamaśōḻapuram for the expenses of Tiruchchannaḍai (procession) and
other items of worship to the deity. These taxes are said to have
been paid as aḍaippam(?) in the shape of 3 kāśu and 30 kalam of
paddy every year by Iruṅgōḷar who held the jīvitam of the nāḍu from
the 25th to the 28th year of the king and the residents found
themselves to pay the same taxes to the temple in future also
irrespective of whether the nāḍu was granted as jīvitam or tirappu to
any person (by the king). It is also stated that these residents received
40 kāśu from the temple as permanent investment for interest.

l.ta:
puṇariy akaḻ cū[ḻ*]nta
puviyil ponnemi aḷavun tannemi naṭappa viḷaṅku
jayamakaḷai iḷaṅkopparuvattu vikkiramat toḻilāl
putumaṇam puṇarntu matuvaraiyiṭṭam vayirākarattu vār
ayil muṉaik kontaḷavaracar tantaḷamiriya vāḷuṟai
kaḻittut toḷvalikāṭṭip porpari naṭāttik kirttiyai
niṟutti vaṭaticai vākai cūḻat teṉticait temarukamalap
pūmakaḷ potumaiyum po-

nniyāṭai naṉṉilappāvai taṉimaiyun tavirttup puṉita-
naṟ tiru maṇimakuṭa murimaiyiṟ cūṭit tannaṭi yiraṇṭun
taṭamuṭiyākat toṉṉila ventar cūṭa muṉṉai maṉuvāṟu
peruka kaliyāṟu vaṟuppa ceṅkol ticai toṟuñ cella
veṇkuṭai iru nila viḷākam veṅkaṇun taṉātu•••
veṇṇilāt tikaḻa oru tanimeruviṟ puliviḷaiyāṭa vār
kaṭaṟṟivāntarattup pūpālar tiṟaivirutantattañcorikaḷiṟu
muṟai muṟai ni-

ṟpa vilaṅkiya teṉṉavan karuntalai paruntalait taṭattana
ponnakar puṟattiṭaik kiṭappap piṉṉāṭ piṟkulap
piṟaipola niṟpiḻaiyeṉṉuñ colletir koṭiṟṟallatu
taṅkai villetir koṭā maṉṉavan [ka]llatar naṅkili
tuṭarntu maṇalūr naṭuveṉat tuṅkapattiraiyaḷavum
veṅkaṇum paṭappaṭar veṅkaḷiṟum viṭṭa tanmāṉamuṅ
kūṟiya viramuṅ kiṭappa eṟiya malaikaḷum mutuku neḷippa
iḻinta netikaḷuñ cuḻaṉṟuṭain toṭa viḻunta kaṭalkaḷu-
ntalaivirit talamarak kuṭaticait tannāḷukantu tānun
tāṉaiyum paṉṉāḷiṭṭa palapala mutukum payattetir māṟiya
jayappe[ru*]ntiruvum paḻiyukantu koṭutta pukaḻuñ celvamum
vāḷāviṭṭa maṭantaiyariṭṭamum miḷātu kuṭutta veṅkari
niṟaiyuṅ kaṅkama-

ṇṭalamuñ ciṅkaṇameṉṉum paraṇiyiraṇṭum oruvicai
kaikkoṇṭaruḷa teṭiya pukaḻoṭu pāṇṭimaṇṭalaṅ
koḷḷat tiruviḷḷat taṭaittu veḷḷavaru parittalaṅkaḷum
porukarittalaṅkaḷu ntantiravāriyum muṭaittāy vantu
vaṭakaṭal teṉkaṭal paṭarvatu polat taṉ peruñceṉaiyai
evip paiñcavar aiyvarum poruta porkkaḷat tañci
veruneḷittoṭi araṇeṉap pukka kāṭarattuṭaittu

nāṭaṭippaṭuttu maṟṟavar tammai vaṉacarar tiriyum pocca
veñcura meṟṟi koṟṟa

m vicaipeṟa
niṟutti viramun tiyākamum niṟuttiya pārmicaic civaniṭattu
maiyeṉat tiṉacintāmaṇip puvaṉa- muḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum
viṟṟiruntaruḷiya kovi

- āvatu
irupattonpatāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattup
perumpāṇappāṭip pulināṭṭu

ṇarum veḷḷāḷarum
uḷḷiṭṭom eṅkaḷ nāṭṭu koyaṟṟūrāṉa uttama
coḻapurattut tiruvakatti

vatāṉamāka inṉāṭṭut tirumalaikkut teṉkiḻakkaṭainta
kāvankuṭṭai yeṉṟu peruṭaiya ūr nāṟpāṟkellai
uṭumpoṭi āmai tavaḻntu kalluṅkaḷḷiyum nāṭṭiṉa
nilattāl vanta iṟai maṉaikku māṭṭukkun taṟiyiṟait
taṭṭārp pāṭṭam uḷḷiṭṭa antarāyaṅkaḷukku yāṇṭu
irupattañcāvatu mutal irupatteṭṭāvatu varai iṉnāṭu
jivitamuṭaiya iruṅkoḷar aṭaippam koṇṭa kācu

ikkācu munṟu nellu muppatiṉ kalamum yāṇṭu
irupattonpatāvatu mutal nāṅkaḷe iṟukka
kaṭavomākavum iruṅkoḷarkkut tavintu iṉnāṭu
maṟṟo[ru]ttaṟku jivitamākilu ntiṟappākilum ivvaṭaippaṭi
mel iṟukkaveṇṭumeṉṉil nāṅkaḷe maṉkuṭi
ivvaṭaippaṭi niṟuttuvomākavum itu
aḻippikkoḷvāruḷarākilum itināl vanta iṟai
nāṅkaḷe eṉṟu koṇṭu iṟukka kaṭavomākavum it
tevar śrī paṇṭārattut tiṅkaṭ kālakkup palicaikku
nāṭṭup potumaiyirukoṇṭa anṟāṭu naṟkācu

varai nāṭṭut tanmamākac celuttak kaṭavomāka
itukku aḻivuceyvārum aḻivu colluvārum

vattip paṭuvārākac
camaiyañ ceytu kuṭuttom pulināṭṭu nāṭṭom puli
nāṭṭu nāṭṭār paṇiyiṉāl eḻutiṉen nāṭṭuk
kaṇakkarāṉa••••••• naṉ ivai eṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku
ivai eṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai naṅkiliyāṉa aṉanta
vikkiramacoḻa catuṟpetimaṅkalattuc coḻanārāyaṇa

mārāyan eḻuttu puṅkanūr vaḷavanārāyaṇac
catuṟperimaṅkalattu pūvaṟti urut

rātittak kiramittaṉ eḻuttu
ācaṅkai cāmuṇṭakkiramittaṉ oppu akuḷi
mātavakkiramittaṉ oppu ippaṭikku aṟiven māḷava
iraviyaṇak kiramittaṉ eḻuttu

koyāṟṟūrāṉa uttama coḻapurattu yirukkum viyāpāri
pampanāṉa uttamacoḻan eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai
kaṅkaikoṇṭa

pulināṭāḻvā••••••• ṭṭu
uppai nelli kaṅkai koṇṭa coḻa puli nāṭāḻvāṉ
oppu ivai uppai nelli veḷāṉ comatevaṉ

pettaiya kāmuṇṭan eḻuttu ippaṭikku

coḻa kāmuṇṭan oppu
ivai muttakkūr irukaiyakāmuṇṭan eḻuttu itu
maṅkalattu

ṇan vaṭukapāṭik kecuvakkiramavittaṉ
oppu ippaṭikku•••••••• vittan oppu ippaṭikku ivai
ceṟālattu āṭkkoṇṭān oppu itu cāmuṇṭayan-
paḷḷi jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻa

mārāyaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku
ivai ciṟuviḷāṅkāṭṭur nākayakāmuṇṭan oppu ivai
maṇikaṭṭi vacavaiya kāmuṇṭan oppu ivai pālantaiy
oppu ivai peruviḷaṅkāṭṭur aḻakiya coḻak kāmuṇṭan
oppu ivai kuḷattūr kalivānaṉ citaiyakāmuṇṭan
eḻuttu ippaṭikku kaṟkaṭṭur jayaṅkoṇṭa ve-

ḷān oppu ippaṭikku ivai murampil vāṇiyar kāmuṇṭan
oppu ippaṭikku ivai veḷiyanūr kaṅkaikoṇṭap-
pallavarātittaṉ oppu ivai kaṟappaḷḷi•••••• koṇṭa
coḻan oppu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 557.
(A.R. No. 557 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, AGASTYAKOṆḌA
Agastyēśvara temple-On the north wall
inscription.p:
This is dated merely in the cyclic year Kīlaka and Āṉi 15. It
registers the appointment of one Sāmagaṉ Dēvaṉ for the post of
uvachchakkāṇi in the temple with the title Tiruvagatti-mārāyan attached
to his name by the authorities (sthānattār) of the temple of
Agattinātar. The post carried with it the enjoyment of some lands
(specified) and house site as remuneration.

l.ta:
kilakavarucattu āṉi mātam [karu ti] akattinātar koyilil
tānattāru[m] śrīmā-

nampi cikāriyañ ceyvikiṟṟavarkaḷum nallanāya[ru]m
cāmakaṉ tevanuk-

ku uvaccukāṇikku tiruvakattimārāya neṉṟu peruṅ
kuṭuttu cattirakkuṭṭaiyi-

kaḻaniyum neṟkuntiyil añ[cu*] kuḷakkollaiyu[m*]
mulattānattukku etir maṟaiyum

akatticu[ra*] nattalum viṭṭappa nallūr nallūr neṟkunti
toṟu uḷḷa paḷḷi

m ivvūril piṭārikoyil pūcitti muttaiyum kuṭuttu
ca

cevikka kaṭavaṉāka cammatittu uvaccukāṇi kuṭuttom
tānattārom [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 558.
(A.R. No. 558 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, AGASTYAKOṆḌA
Agastyēśvara temple-On the west wall
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 1298, Ānanda (mistake for Śaka 1296)
(Text not available)

inscription.head:
No. 559.
(A.R. No. 559 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, AGASTYAKOṆḌA
Agastyēśvara temple-On the base of the west wall
Tribh. Vīrarājēndra-Chōḷadēva 14th year
inscription.p:
This records the consecration of the image of Saint Tiruunāvukkaraśu-
dēvar in the temple of Tiruvagattīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār by Seyya
Nambirāṭṭiyār, the wife of the chief Uttamaśōḻa-Gaṅgan alias Śelva-
Gaṅga who receives the epithets Kuvaḷālapura-paramēśvara Gaṅgakulōttama,
Kāvērīvallabha and Nandagirinātha. The inscription seems to be one of
Kulōttuṅga III.

l.ta:
- āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭa
coḻamaṇṭalattu vaṭapuli [n]āṭṭut tiruvakatticcuramuṭaiya
nāyaṉārkku kuvalālapura parame

raṉ kaṅkakulottamaṉ kāverivallavaṉ nantakirinātaṉ
uttamacoḻak kaṅkarāṉa celva kaṅkar nampirāṭṭiyār
jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻa

eḻuntaruḷun tevarkaḷil tirunāvukkaracu tevarai
eḻuntaruḷivittu tiru

inscription.p:
Seems to refer to some gift by the praja of Krottakōṭa and
Immaḍi-Chīkarālu, for conducting the car festival to god Agastyēśvara.

l.te:
sva[sti śrī] jayābhyudaya sālivāhana saka varuṣaṁbulu 1577 ·agunēṭi
manmatha saṁvatsara vaiśākha ba 12 lu śrīmatu ·agastēśvara svāmiki
yēṭāṭikinni tappaka rathōtsava dha¨rmaṁ naḍipiṁci [vo] niyya krottakōṭa
sīmareḍlu prajalu magaṭūriyiṁmmaḍi cikarālu tiṁmmayagānivārikiṁnni
nāḍigelōna • • • puṭlu koni ni¨rnaya peṭṭukonna vivara

inscription.head:
No. 561.
(A.R. No. 561 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, AGASTYAKOṆḌA
Agastyēśvara temple-On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in the front
Śelva-Gaṅga, son of Uttamachōḷa-Gaṅga Padumadēva-Śaka 1[1]51
inscription.p:
The chief receives among others the titles Mahāmaṇḍalēśvaraṉ,
Maṇḍalika-Dēvēndraṉ Saptāṅga Gaṅgan and Saṅgrāmadhīraṉ. The
inscription stops with the mention of the Śaka year. Some piece is
evidently missing as in the Report. It records the gift of an ornament
by Śrīpatināyaṉ, son of the king (Śelva-Gaṅga).

l.ta: ntikiri nātaṉ maṇṭalika teventira cattiya vā
mecca meccātā[ra*] kaṇṭaṉ uttamacoḻak kaṅkaṉ patu-
ma tevar makanār celvakaṅkaṉeṉ uṭaiyār tiruvaka-

inscription.head:
No. 562.
(A.R. No. 562 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, AGASTYAKOṆḌA
Agastyēśvara temple-On the base of the south wall of the maṇḍapa
right of entrance, first tier.
inscription.p:
The chief Uttamachōḷa-Gaṅga alias Vīra-Gaṅga Vēṭṭum Amarābaraṇaṉ
Vanneñjaṉ is said to have made a gift of 1200 kuḻi of land under the
Naṅgili tank, to the temple of Tiruvagattīśvaram-uḍaiyār, for the
requirements of a service in the temple.

l.ta:
āḷuṭaiyaṉāyaṉār tiruvakatticura
muṭaiyārkku uttama coḻakaṅkaṉāṉa virakaṅkaṉ
veṭṭumamarāparaṇaṉ vaṉṉeñ-

caṉ oru cantikku naṅkili erikiḻak kuṟṟilaṉ paṟṟil viṭṭa
nilam āyirattirunūṟu kuḻi itai māṟṟuvāṉ keṅkai
kumariyiṭaip pāvattil [paṭuvāṉ*]

inscription.head:
No. 563.
(A.R. No. 563 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, AGASTYAKOṆḌA
Agastyēśvara temple-On the base of the south wall of the maṇḍapa
second, third and fourth tiers
Rājarājadēva (III)-19th year (1234 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of two cows for a lamp in the temple by
one Sūranāyanār alias Śīya-Gaṅgar and another two cows by a lady by
name Uḍaiyanāchchi.

l.ta:
ku cūra nāyanārāna ciyakaṅkar cantiviḷakkonṟukku āvaṇi
mācam viṭ-

ṭa pacu iraṇṭu u[ṭaiyāṉ] uyyavantāḷ makaḷ uṭaiya-
nācci cantiviḷak-

konṟukku viṭṭa pacu iraṇṭum kaikkoṇṭu
cantirāccavarai celu-

ttakkaṭavomāṉoṅ kaṟaikka[ṇi] paṭṭaṉun tampi paṭṭanun
kā-

inscription.head:
No. 564.
(A.R. No. 564 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, AGASTYAKOṆḌA
Agastyēśvara temple-On the base of the Dakshiṇāmūrti-maṇḍapa
inscription.p:
This records a gift by one Vaḍugappiḷḷai alias Toṇḍaimāṇ, son of
Avaiyampukkāṉ Vāṇarāyar of Murugamuchchi, of all the land which
was irrigated by a well, south of the hill, stated to have been
constructed by the donor himself-as tirunāmattukkāṇi to god
Tiruvagattīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyanār.

l.ta:
śrī murukamucci avaiyampukkānāṉa
vāṇarāyar makan vaṭukappuḷḷai-

kku teṟku nāṉ kaṭṭina toṇṭikkiḻ••• cariyil
nirpāñcuviḷaiyum nilam aṭaṅka•••

lum cantirātittavarai cella•••• tirunāmattuk
kāṇiyāka kuṭutten toṇ[ṭai]mā(na)ṉeṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 565.
(A.R. No. 564 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, AGASTYAKOṆḌA
Agastyēśvara temple-On the same base
inscription.p:
This is built in the middle. It is a Tamil verse in praise of
Adigēśaṉ, the Adigaimān chief.

l.ta:
vetam moḻiyatikecakaṟkiṟait taṉaiyuñ
cenam malai[ya]c ceka taṇṭamuṭṭa vivaṉ enta pātam
paṇiyun tirukkalla vovvā pāvaṇaiye .

inscription.head:
No. 566.
(A.R. No. 566 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, AGASTYAKOṆḌA
On a boulder called the Durga-guṇḍu on the hill
Uttamachōḷa-Gaṅga alias Śelva Gaṅga.
inscription.p:
It seems to record a gift of variśai (contribution?) for the
maintenance of a maṭha by Śevaliyaṉ Vēṭṭa (Pāḍi?)vaḷavaṉ, son of
Vikkiyāṇḍai, a vēḷaikkāṟa of the chief. Below this is another
inscription recording the faithfulness (in following up the above gift?)
by one Dēvamōgaṉ, son of Dēvapperumāḷ to this (Pāḍi?)vaḷavaṉ (It is
also possible that both the inscriptions record oaths of fealty to their
respective masters).

edition.p:
A few letters are engraved both above and below this, but they are damaged
and difficult to make out.

l.note:
The above is enclosed within the four lines of a rectangle with a chauri figured
outside. Below this are the five lines of the next inscription.
On either side of this is a triple canspied umbrella.

inscription.head:
No. 567.
(A.R. No. 567 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, MIṬṬAPALLE
On a boulder in the field
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Uttamachōḷa-Gaṅga alias Śelva-Gaṅga.
inscription.p:
This records a grant, by the chief, of half the lands in the
village Neṟkundi as dēvadāna to the temple of Agattīśvaram-uḍaiya-
nāyanār for the celebration of the festival in his name in the temple
in the month of Māśi.

inscription.head:
No. 568.
(A.R. No. 568 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, MINIKI
On a rock called the Minikibaṇḍa near the village
Kulōttuṅga Chōḷa (I)-39th year (1108 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This begins with a passage (in faulty Sanskrit) consisting of some
birudas such as Jāhnavīkulāmala, Khaḍgaikaprahāra, Koṅkuṇipadma and
Padmamahādhirāja, and followed by the introduction Pugaḻmādu
viḷaṅga, etc. of king Kulōttuṅga I who is called here Vīra-Rājēndra-
Chōḷa. But preceding his name and regnal year are given the titles
Paramaṇḍalāditya, Virudarājabhayaṅkara, Dushṭaragaṇḍa, Rājēndraśōḻa-
kshatriya, Gaṅganuḻambaṉ and Muḷvāy-maṇḍalikaṉ, introducing the chief
Sokkanāyaṉ, son of Nuḷambadēvar. This feudatory is said to have
constructed a channel from the big tank at Uppanelli in Mudukurukki
situated in Vaḍapuli-nāḍu a division of Perumbāṇappāḍi in Nigariliśōḻa
maṇḍalam. His son Nuḷambadēvaṉ claims to have improved this
channel.

l.gr:
svasti śrī [||*] svasti jidam bhagavatā gatapunat
ggana[tā]bhina patmanābhina śrīmat jatnavikulāmala
śrīkha[ḍa]gaikaprahāra gaṇḍa ta-
ri gaṇōbhadāra śrīmat kōṅkuṇi patma patma

l.ta:
pa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ puṇara urimaiyiṟ ciṟaṉta
maṇimuṭi cū-

ṭi villavar nilaikeṭa minavar kulaitara āṉai maṉnavar
ikaliḻaṉ timi-
lattup perum pāṇappāṭi vaṭapuli nāṭṭu mutuku-
ḷampa tevaneṉ caccaraṉ meṭṭi tāmalūr maṉnavaṉ

āvai kāvilaṉ aracamikāmaṉ kāvalar pukaḻ noṭitaṉ
t[e]kka(ṇ)-

ṇṭaṉ kaṭṭiya kaḻaṟkāl tuṭṭarātitta ṉaya muyaṉṟacaccaṉ
meṭṭi

muṉivar kovaṉ muḷvāyṉuḷampaṉ cokkanāyaṉ
kaṇ[ṭi]kāvala ali-

yeṉṟu vilaṅkap pecinār ūrākil keṅkaik karaiyiṟ
kurāṟ pa-

inscription.head:
No. 569.
(A.R. No. 569 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, BŪḌIDIPALLE
On a slab set up in a field behind the ruined village
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, part I, No. 2.

inscription.head:
No. 570.
(A.R. No. 570 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, BŪḌIDIPALLE
On a slab set up (another slab) in a field behind the ruined village
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, part I, No. 3.

inscription.head:
No. 571.
(A.R. No. 571 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, BŪḌIDIPALLE
On a slab (third slab) set up in a field behind the ruined village
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, part I, No. 4.

inscription.head:
No. 572.
(A.R. No. 572 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, BŪḌIDIPALLE
On a slab set up infront of the Puṇḍīśvara temple
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, part II, No. 428.

inscription.head:
No. 573.
(A.R. No. 573 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, MŪGAVĀḌI
Puṇḍīśvara temple-On a second slab set up in its front
Parak. Adhirājēndradēva-3rd year (1070 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It begins with the introduction Tiṅgaḷēr-
malarndu etc. It is stated that the officer Vichchādiran Madhurāntakaṉ
alias Sēnāpati Vīrarājēndra-Kārāṇai-Viḻupparaiyaṉ set up the images
of Sōmāskandadēva called Kārāṇai-Viṭaṅkar after his name, and of
Vighnēśvara under the name Arumoḻi-nāyakar in the temple of
Tirumukkīśvaram-udaiya-mahādēva at Mukkaipāḍi in Puli-nāḍu a division
of Perumbāṇappāḍi in Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam and
endowed 4000 kuḻi of land under the tank Vīrarājēndrappērēri. The
mēlvāram of both the kār and paśānam crops from this land was to
be measured out to the temple by the Ūrār of the village towards all
the specified requirements of worship and offerings to these deities on
ordinary and special festival days throughout the year.

inscription.head:
No. 574.
(A.R. No. 574 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, MŪGAVĀḌI
Puṇḍīśvara temple-On a stone forming the roof of a small shrine in
the courtyard
Parak. Rājēndradēva. Date lost.
inscription.p:
This is a fragmentary and damaged inscription containing only
the beginning of the introduction.

- la viracoḻaṉ•• lāl pukaḻ kalikāla co

inscription.head:
No. 575.
(A.R. No. 575 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, YĀṬAVĀKILI
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-On the base of the temple
Parak. Rājēndrachōḷa I—22nd year (A.D. 1036)
inscription.p:
This begins with the introduction Tirumaṉṉivaḷara, etc. It records
a gift of 500 kuḻi of land to the east of Kūttāḍi-ēri (tank) by a
Veḷḷāḷa named Kāri Maṇikkāḍa-Gāmuṇḍan for the avirbali and archana
worship in the temple of Iruṅgīśvaram-uḍaiya-Mahādēva which he had
constructed in his village Ēṭṭavāykkāl in Puli-nāḍu, a division of
Perumbāṇappāḍi in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. The tank also is stated to
have been the work of this donor.

inscription.head:
No. 576.
(A.R. No. 576 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, YĀṬAVĀKILI
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-On the walls of the temple
Rājarāja-Chōḷadēva (III)-29th year, Viśvāvasu (1245 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This seems to record a gift of some land for repairs (?) to the
temple of Irugīśvaram-uḍaiyār by Dēvāṇḍai and Irukkāṇḍai, the
descendants of the founder of the temple, who is here called Kumbaḷa-
gāmunḍa. Mention is made of Śelva-Gaṅgar during whose administration
this gift was probably made.

inscription.head:
No. 577.
(A.R. No. 577 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, YĀṬAVĀKILI
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-On a slab set up in the court yard
Kampaṇa-Uḍaiyār (II)-Jaya (Śaka 1276 or 1354 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It records the gift of the village Eṭṭavāykkāl,
excluding the lands therein which had been already granted to others
as sthānamānyam, to the temple of Tiruvēṅgaḍa-nāyaṉār as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam
by Sōmappar under the orders of Kampaṇa-uḍaiyar. The actual ruler at
the time was probably Bukka I.

edition.h4:
Piece 1 These pieces are also found in the same bundle, but they are not clearly
connected with the above or with one another.

inscription.head:
No. 578.
(A.R. No. 578 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, EMBĀḌI
On a vīragal set up in the village
inscription.p:
It records the death of Irumāṇayya-gāvuṇḍa on the occasion of
the capture of the town of Uppanelli. In early characters.

Records the death of a hero named Madahariyāḷ on the occasion
of the capture of the town of Uppanelli. In early characters.

Seems to refer to the rule of Vajjaḷadēva over Puli-nāḍu while
Ballaha was the ruling king. In early characters.

inscription.head:
No. 581.
(A.R. No. 581 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, EMBĀḌI
On the fourth vīragal (hero stone) in the village
inscription.p:
Records the death of Khaḻa-guḍi-gāvuṇḍa on the occasion of the
capture of Uppanelli. In late characters.
1 Evidently unfinished.

Records the death of an individual alongwith Śivayya, on the
occasion of the destruction of the village Uppanelli. In early characters.

Records the death of Poḻaliseṭṭi and his brother-in-law in the
course of the destruction of the village Uppanelli. In early characters.

inscription.head:
No. 584.
(A.R. No. 584 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, EMBĀḌI
On a stone set up in the village
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, part I, No. 10.

inscription.head:
No. 585.
(A.R. No. 585 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, EMBĀḌI
On a slab lying in front of a private house
Parak. Rājēndra-Chōḷa I-5th year and 23rd year.
inscription.p:
This records an order of the king passed in the 5th year of his
reign while he was seated in the eastern hall called Madhurāntakan of
his palace at Paḻaiyāṟu and said to have been confirmed in his 23rd
year and 302nd day, fixing the amount of kāṇikkaḍaṉ due to the king
every year by the residents of Perumāṭṭi in Puli-nāḍu a division of
Perumbāṇappāḍi in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. These latter had got the
village from other hands free of all restrictive conditions of tenancy.
The date of the final execution of the order is given at the end of
the record as the 25th year and 302nd day, perhaps by mistake for
the 23rd year only.

inscription.head:
No. 586.
(A.R. No. 586 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, EḌAPAÑJANI,
Kāśi-Viśvēśvara temple-On the base of the verandah in its front
inscription.p:
This consists of disjointed pieces of two inscriptions one being
that of Kulōttuṅga I with the introduction Pugaḻsūḻnda-puṇari, etc.

The first piece is dated in the 29th year of the king. One of the
pieces mentions a kāṇiyāḷan of Karkaḍūr in Puli-nāḍu a division of
Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. Another refers to one Rājēndramallar and
Śrīpatināyaṉ, the donor of an ornament to the deity, while a third
piece makes mention of a gift of 400 kuḻi of land under the big tank
by Vikramaśōḻa-gāmuṇḍan and Nāgayaṉ. It is not known to whose
reigns these are to be assigned.

inscription.head:
No. 587.
(A.R. No. 587 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, CHITTOOR TALUK, MOGILI
Mogilīśvara temple-On a slab set up within the temple premises
Rājēndradēva-6th year (1057-8 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of land as dēvadāna to the temple of
Tirumāṇiyūṟal-nāyanār at Muḻibil (Mogili) by a merchant named
Niyamaṅkiḻār-chakravarttigaḷ. He is stated have reclaimed the land for
cultivation.

inscription.head:
No. 588.
(A.R. No. 588 of 1906)
CHITTOOR DISTRICT, CHITTOOR TALUK, MOGILI
Mogilīśvara temple-On a second slab set up in the court yard
Vīra Bukkarāya (II) son of Harihararāya,
Pārttiva (Śaka 1327 = 1405-6 A.D.)
inscription.p:
The inscription is much damaged. It seems to record a gift of
land to the temple for the requirements of offerings to the deity. The
donor's name is not clear. Mention is made of Kampaṇṇa-uḍaiyar.
